summary: R3 systems handoff resolves the qa_reviewer P1 finding that R2 command evidence was not reproducible after lead committed lifecycle route/status state. The command evidence now uses a versioned R3 JSON/status probe that accepts the valid lifecycle states review_ready_r3 and review_handoff_committed_r3 for systems, and awaiting_r3_review and reviewing_r3 for QA. It still verifies the corrected main hash, absence of the invalid full hash in cited evidence files, M3_GATE marker, task route, R3 systems handoff shape, schema version, and allowed dirty paths. M3_GATE remains PASS pending independent qa_reviewer acceptance and lead integration; no M4 work was started.
commit: a48f9383 (origin/chore/m3-exit-gate HEAD before R3 writer fixes; current R3 review evidence is uncommitted for lead integration)

M3-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 is REVIEW; M3 dependency tasks remain CLOSED.
No output; no M4 task was started.

decisions: M3_GATE remains PASS, pending independent qa_reviewer acceptance and lead integration.
The R2 JSON/status probe is superseded by the lifecycle-aware R3 probe in project/messages/outbox/M3-EXIT-VALIDATION-001__command-evidence.md.
The R3 probe deliberately accepts both writer-ready and lead-committed lifecycle states for R3, while rejecting unrelated R1/R2/request-change statuses.
No product, package, content-source, tool, CI, ADR, roadmap, acceptance-matrix, autonomous-goal doc, manifest, checksum, or validation-report path was modified.
No Human Gate or M4 start is introduced by this R3 correction.
risks: Formal M3 acceptance still depends on independent qa_reviewer R3 acceptance and lead integration.
Earlier R1/R2 handoffs remain audit history; this R3 handoff is the current routed systems evidence after QA request changes.
requested_action: Re-review the R3 lifecycle-aware command evidence. The exact command in command-evidence.md should exit 0 both before and after lead commits the R3 review route state. If accepted, write a valid qa_reviewer handoff routed to lead_orchestrator. Do not start M4 from this review.
